skill.name: cold-email
skill.description: Write B2B cold emails and follow-up sequences that get replies. Use when the user wants to write cold outreach emails, prospecting emails, cold email campaigns, sales development emails, or SDR emails. Also use when the user mentions "cold outreach," "prospecting email," "outbound email," "email to leads," "reach out to prospects," "sales email," "follow-up email sequence," "nobody's replying to my emails," or "how do I write a cold email." Covers subject lines, opening lines, body copy, CTAs, personalization, and multi-touch follow-up sequences. For warm/lifecycle email sequences, see email-sequence. For sales collateral beyond emails, see sales-enablement.
skill.summary: Write effective B2B cold emails and follow-up sequences that sound human and get responses
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: This is a benign marketing skill providing cold email writing guidance. All 19 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner incorrectly flagged common marketing/business words (leverage, research, signals, requests) as security threats. No actual security risks present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns. Investigation shows this is marketing content using business terms like 'leverage', 'proof point', 'framework'. No cryptographic code present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Content is marketing guidance - the scanner incorrectly matched business terminology with cryptographic terms

low_findings.title: False Positive: System/Network Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'reconnaissance' patterns. These are marketing research terms like 'research signals', 'investigate', 'prospecting' - not security scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Terms like 'research signals', 'investigate prospect' are standard B2B sales vocabulary, not security threats

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Detection
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at SKILL.md:15. This is a file path reference (.agents/product-marketing-context.md), not shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill instructs checking for a local context file path, which is standard agent skill pattern - no command execution

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Detection
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'Python HTTP libraries' at SKILL.md:51. This is the word 'requests' in a marketing context ('meeting requests'), not HTTP code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text discusses 'meeting requests' and CTAs - standard email marketing terminology, not network code

content.user_title: Write B2B Cold Emails That Get Replies
content.value_statement: Cold emails often sound like templates and get ignored. This skill teaches you to write concise, peer-like emails that connect with prospects and trigger responses using proven frameworks and personalization techniques.
actual_capabilities: Write subject lines that get emails opened (2-4 words, lowercase, internal-looking)
Create personalized opening lines that connect to prospect's actual situation
Apply copywriting frameworks (PAS, BAB, AIDA, QVC) to different scenarios
Build 3-5 email follow-up sequences with new value in each touch
Calibrate voice and tone for different audiences (C-suite, technical, mid-level)
Identify and fix common cold email mistakes in existing drafts
limitations: Does not provide verified prospect contact data or email addresses
Does not send or automate emails - only assists with writing
Does not integrate with email platforms (Gmail, Outlook, etc.)
Cannot verify if email content complies with CAN-SPAM or GDPR regulations

use_cases.title: Write a new cold outreach campaign
use_cases.description: Create cold emails from scratch for a B2B product targeting specific roles and industries
use_cases.target_user: SDRs, sales development reps, founders doing outbound

use_cases.title: Improve reply rates on existing emails
use_cases.description: Analyze and rewrite underperforming cold emails to increase opens and responses
use_cases.target_user: Sales teams with low response rates

use_cases.title: Build follow-up sequences
use_cases.description: Create multi-touch follow-up cadences that add value with each email

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cold Email Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a cold email to [role] at [company type]. We sell [product] that helps with [problem]. Our main proof point: [specific result metric].

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this cold email and tell me what's wrong. Then rewrite it following cold email best practices: [paste email]

prompt_templates.title: Follow-Up Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a follow-up sequence for a prospect who didn't reply to my first email about [topic]. How many emails should I send, what should each say, and how far apart should they be?

prompt_templates.title: Subject Line Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best subject lines for cold emails to [role] in [industry]? Give me 5 examples that are short, boring, and look internal.

output_examples.input: Write a cold email to VP Marketing at B2B SaaS companies about content analytics software
output_examples.output:
Subject: content attribution

Saw your recent post on content ROI - resonates with what we're hearing from marketing leaders.

Most teams we talk to can't actually prove which blog posts drive pipeline. They have traffic data but no revenue connection.

One SaaS company we worked with: they discovered their top-of-funnel posts were actually hurting conversion. Switched strategy based on attribution data, saw 3x content-attributed revenue in 90 days.

Worth a quick chat about how they're thinking about this?

-

output_examples.input: Review this email: 'Dear Sir, I hope this email finds you well. We are a leading provider of innovative solutions. We have helped 500+ companies. Would you like to schedule a call?'
output_examples.output:
Issues identified:
- Generic greeting (no personalization)
- Filler phrase ('I hope this email finds you well')
- 'Leading provider of innovative solutions' is jargon
- No specific value or proof point
- Too aggressive CTA (30-min call in first touch)

Rewritten version focuses on prospect's world, includes specific proof, uses low-friction CTA.

best_practices: Write like a peer, not a vendor - use contractions, keep it conversational
Every sentence must earn its place - cold email is ruthlessly short
Personalization must connect to the problem - remove it and the email should still make sense
anti_patterns: Opening with 'I hope this email finds you well' or 'My name is X'
Asking for 30-minute calls in the first touch email

faq.question: What makes a good cold email subject line?
faq.answer: Good subject lines are 2-4 words, lowercase, and look internal. They should sound like they came from a colleague, not a vendor. Avoid emojis, ALL CAPS, and prospect's first name.

faq.question: How many follow-up emails should I send?
faq.answer: Send 3-5 follow-ups with increasing gaps between them. Each should add new value - a different angle, fresh proof, or useful resource. Not just 'just checking in'.

faq.question: What is low-friction CTA?
faq.answer: Low-friction CTAs are soft asks like 'Worth exploring?' or 'Would this be useful?' instead of 'Book a demo' or 'Schedule a call.' Easy to respond to with a one-line reply.

faq.question: How do I personalize cold emails effectively?
faq.answer: Use the 4-level system: 1) Basic (name, company), 2) Contextual (role, industry), 3) Research-based (recent posts, funding, hires), 4) Problem-specific (identified pain point). Level 3-4 outperform templates.

faq.question: What voice should I use for C-suite recipients?
faq.answer: For C-suite, use ultra-brief, peer-level, understated tone. Get to the point fast. Lead with their world, not your product. One short paragraph max.

faq.question: What's the difference between cold email and email sequence?
faq.answer: Cold email is unsolicited outbound outreach to prospects who haven't opted in. Email sequence (or lifecycle email) is for leads who have already engaged - downloaded a whitepaper, signed up, etc. Use cold email skill for outreach, email-sequence skill for nurturing.
